Class Event

java.lang.Object
planner.model.Event
Direct Known Subclasses:
RecurringEvent
public class Event extends Object
Event is the base domain model class representing an event in the calendar system.

Design Pattern - Inheritance Preparation: The attributes use the 'protected' access modifier, which encapsulates the data from external classes while allowing subclasses (such as RecurringEvent) to have direct access to inherited attributes. This enables proper object-oriented design with inheritance hierarchies.

Design Pattern - Composition: The Event class contains a list of Attendee objects, establishing a strong structural relationship (one-to-many) between an event and its participants. This allows multiple attendees to be associated with each event.

Features:

  • Core event information: title, date/time, location, description, category
  • Reminder system with configurable minutes before event
  • Attendee management with composition pattern
  • CSV serialization for data persistence with input sanitization
  • Formatted date/time display for user interface
Version:
2.0
Author:
Gustavo Henrique Nogueira de Andrade Filho, Pedro Rocha Dantas, Leonardo Oliveira Eid

  • Constructor Details

    • Event

      public Event(String title, LocalDateTime dateTime, String location, String description, String category, int reminderMinutesBefore)
      Constructs an Event with the specified details.

      Initializes a new event with all core information. The attendee list is initialized internally as an empty ArrayList to prevent NullPointerException when accessing the attendees collection.

      Parameters:
      title - The title or name of the event (mandatory)
      dateTime - The LocalDateTime when the event occurs
      location - The physical or virtual location of the event
      description - A detailed description, notes, or additional information about the event
      category - The category or classification of the event
      reminderMinutesBefore - The number of minutes before the event to trigger a reminder

  • Method Details

    • getTitle

      public String getTitle()
      Returns the event's title.
      Returns:
      The String representing the event title

    • setTitle

      public void setTitle(String title)
      Sets the event's title.
      Parameters:
      title - The new title for the event

    • getDateTime

      public LocalDateTime getDateTime()
      Returns the event's date and time.
      Returns:
      The LocalDateTime representing when the event occurs

    • setDateTime

      public void setDateTime(LocalDateTime dateTime)
      Sets the event's date and time.
      Parameters:
      dateTime - The new LocalDateTime for the event

    • getLocation

      public String getLocation()
      Returns the event's location.
      Returns:
      The String representing the event location

    • setLocation

      public void setLocation(String location)
      Sets the event's location.
      Parameters:
      location - The new location for the event

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Returns the event's description.
      Returns:
      The String containing the event description

    • setDescription

      public void setDescription(String description)
      Sets the event's description.
      Parameters:
      description - The new description for the event

    • getCategory

      public String getCategory()
      Returns the event's category.
      Returns:
      The String representing the event category (e.g., "Meeting", "Birthday")

    • setCategory

      public void setCategory(String category)
      Sets the event's category.
      Parameters:
      category - The new category for the event

    • getReminderMinutesBefore

      public int getReminderMinutesBefore()
      Returns the reminder time in minutes before the event.
      Returns:
      The number of minutes before the event to display a reminder notification

    • setReminderMinutesBefore

      public void setReminderMinutesBefore(int reminderMinutesBefore)
      Sets the reminder time in minutes before the event.
      Parameters:
      reminderMinutesBefore - The number of minutes before the event to trigger a reminder

    • getAttendees

      public List<Attendee> getAttendees()
      Returns the list of attendees for this event.
      Returns:
      A List of Attendee objects associated with this event

    • addAttendee

      public void addAttendee(Attendee attendee)
      Adds an attendee to the event's attendee list.

      Adds the provided attendee to the internal composition list if the attendee object is not null. This method is part of the composition pattern implementation.

      Parameters:
      attendee - The Attendee object to be added to the event. If null, the operation is silently ignored

    • getFormattedDateTime

      public String getFormattedDateTime()
      Returns the event's date and time in a user-friendly formatted string.

      The format is: "MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm" (e.g., "06/21/2026 14:30")

      Returns:
      A formatted String representation of the date and time

    • toCSV

      public String toCSV()
      Converts the event to a CSV (comma-separated values) format string for file persistence.

      Data Sanitization: This method applies comprehensive input sanitization to all text fields to prevent delimiter conflicts during file I/O operations. The primary delimiter used in the CSV format is the semicolon (;), and the secondary delimiter for attendees is the pipe (|). Newline characters in descriptions are converted to spaces to maintain single-line format.

      CSV Format: The output format is: Title;DateTime;Location;Description;Category;Reminder;Attendees

      Attendee Serialization: Multiple attendees are concatenated with commas, where each attendee is serialized using Attendee.toCSV().

      Returns:
      A formatted String in CSV format ready for storage to disk

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the event for display in the user interface.

      Design Pattern - Polymorphism: This method overrides the Object.toString() method, providing a custom textual representation tailored for UI components and logging.

      The format is: "Title (MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm) - Category"

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A String representation in the format "Title (Date and Time) - Category"
